message:
fix(dns): make cache lookups case-insensitive per RFC 4343

DNS names are case-insensitive (RFC 4343), but the cache was using
raw string keys, causing separate entries for e.g. "Example.COM" vs
"example.com". This wasted memory and caused cache misses when the
application used different casing than the resolver returned.

Add normalize_host_key() that lowercases the host string using
Cow<str> for zero-allocation on the common path (already lowercase).
All cache operations (get_ip, put_ip, remove) now go through
normalization before accessing the HashMap.

Includes test cache_lookup_is_case_insensitive verifying that
"Example.COM", "example.com", and "EXAMPLE.COM" all resolve to
the same cache entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
